rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERWIN FONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #9, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/04/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) A supplemental proof of service showing that a copy of the amended petition has been provided to the reappointed counsel for the conservatee must be filed. 2) In light of the concerns raised and change of circumstances described in the assigned court investigator's report, a supplement addressing the issue of whether the conservatee should be required to continue to pay 100% of garbage, water, insurance, and property taxes must be filed. It should be noted that the Court's 12/28/2011 did not require the conservatee to pay 100% property taxes.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE J. MALINOWSKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/4/2021. 1) Petitioner has not filed an allowance/rejection of the $113.61 creditor claim. 2) Petitioner has not filed a proof of satisfaction of the $113.61 creditor claim. 3) Please provide legal authority in support of the petitioner paying her own creditor claims. A creditor's claim of the personal representative or the attorney must be noted as such. Such a claim must be processed as provided in Probate Code 9252, notwithstanding authority to act under IAEA. [LRSF 14.29]. 4) Please provide legal authority and explain how the Francis Grillo disclaimer is effective. (Probate Code 279.) 5) Please list all issue and heirs-at-law of Francis Grillo and their ages. If there are any issue they must receive notice. (Probate Code 1202). If there are any issue no waiver of account has been filed and an account must be filed. 6) Please provide legal authority and explain how the Patricia Hand Amaro disclaimer is effective. (Probate Code 279.) 7) Please list all issue and heirs-at-law of Patricia Hand Amaro and their ages. If there are any issue they must receive notice. (Probate Code 1202). If there are any issue no waiver of account has been filed and an account must be filed. 8) Please file legal authority in support of administrator having Robert Hand and Lisa Grillo Ciliento sign a General Release and Indemnity Agreement in a probate to receive a preliminary distribution. 9) Please file legal authority and explain the agreements. 10) Please file legal authority in support of the preliminary distributions. 11) Receipts for any preliminary distribution must be on file prior to the approval of final distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) c.] 12) Please explain why there are no assets for distribution when the I&A's alleged $146,245.89 in assets. 13) Please explain how much Sidney Amaro was paid outside the estate. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE PAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/21. (No earlier availability.) The "declarations" filed on 3/23/21 did not fully address the issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 2/2/21 but nonetheless AMENDED a portion the petition. An amending pleading (regardless of how it is entitled) requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including using the proper, mandatory Judicial Council form of Notice of Hearing and providing at least 15 days' notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of a proper amendment for the first accounting period that is fully supported by original bank statements, etc., and proof of service of that amended first accounting petition must be received by the Examiner by no later than 5/7/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYNE THOMAS PETERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/2021. Issues: (to be addressed before hearing) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. (Probate Code 8113). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Whether to reduce the Executor compensation by $396.00 for payment made for a 1-hour consultation with a probate attorney. 2) Attorney requested $7,840.00 in extraordinary compensation. Examiner requested timesheets for ordinary services so the court can determine whether extraordinary fees are "just and reasonable" (Prob. C. 10811(a)). Attorney alleges they did not keep timesheets for ordinary services. 3) Attorney is requesting $3,763.08 in costs. Attorney alleges $1,554.50 was spent on Swift Attorney Services for filing and delivery fees that could have been avoided by mailing documents and filing with file and serve express. 4) Whether the estate should have paid for entry 10/6/2018 Dinner for 34 people plus tip $2,501.05. 5) $6.00 was reimbursed to Gail Yoshida for parking. Parking is absorbed as part of fees [LRSF 14.58 B.] 6) Examiner requested a receipt for entry 5/23 Estevan Lopez Landscaping for $250.00. Petitioner has not provided a receipt.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY** CONSERVATORSHIP OF BRADLEY A. PAKULA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/11/2021. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No current status report is on file. 2) The Ally bank statement filed on 12/31/2020 does not appear to be an original statement. If the conservator received the statement in electronic form, a verification required by Probate Code section 2620(c)(1) must be filed. 3) The current bond must be increased before this account can be approved. Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 03/18/2021 re the pending ex parte petition to increase the bond. 4) Please explain why the GLIC life insurance policy has not been inventoried and appraised. 5) Schedule C reports payment of attorney fees in the amount of $3,301.70 on 05/08/2018, before the Court approved the fees. 6) Schedule E does not list non-cash assets.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIK B. FAALAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although there was no Attachment 27 to the petition (the bank statements were filed separately), the copy of the I&A attached to the pleading was incomplete, the refunds were $392.86, not $932.86, the property on hand in paragraph 24 was not the ending balances shown in the accounting (or, in the case of the Bank of America account xxx4520, not verified by any bank statement either), but as the amounts were higher, no further amendment will be required. The executor was appointed on 12/19/18, but the order was not filed until 12/20/18, and Letters Testamentary (not of Administration) were issued on 2/15/19. Director of Health Care Services. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/21. (No earlier availability.) The second supplement was not signed and verified by the administrator. [CRC 7.103(c).] Prob. C. Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has PREVIIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. The allegations required by Prob. C. Section 1064 apply to a REPORT accompanying a petition and only the provisions applicable to accountings do not have to be addressed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 5/7/21.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 1998 JANICE D. SURLES REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assests
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/18/2021. 1) Notice of the hearing must be on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. 2) Re paragraph 2 of the petition, please provide additional facts, other than the petitioner's residence, establishing that the day-to-day activity of the trust is carried out in San Francisco. 3) Did the settlor die testate? If so, please provide a copy of her will. 4) Has a personal representative been appointed for administration of the settlor's estate? If so, please provide a certified copy of letters issued to the personal representative. 5) Please identify all individuals who would be the settlor's heirs. If any heir is not a beneficiary of the trust, the heir must be served in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a)(2). 6) Is the date (06/19/2020) set forth in paragraph 7 accurate? 7) Is the first label number set forth in paragraph 8 accurate? 8) Please provide a copy of a "Certification of Trust" referred to in paragraph 7. 9) Please provide a copy of the LPL account statement for a period including 08/20/2020. 10) Please provide a copy of the certificate of title to the mobile home. 11) Direct notice must be given to Joshua Murphy. CRC 7.51(a)(2).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN PARKER AKZAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person. [CRC 7.51(a).] Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENNETH CARMAN WRIGHT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/27/2021 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 03/29/2021). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 03/29/2021.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ZUBAIDA BEGUM SHEIKH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/4/2021. 1) Please explain if this is an ancillary probate. 2) The Will does not appear to be an original and is a copy that does not appear to be self proving under Probate Code 8220. Please provide legal authority for the court admitting this Will. 3) Please explain how the petition conform to Probate Code 12520 et seq. 4) Please provide the deceased spouse information on page 4 under #8. 5) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 4/19/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STUART SCOTT KOSITS

rulings.Rulings: continued to 4/27/21 at counsel's request.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YUKIKO ALTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: continued to 5/11/21. no proof of publication on file. no proof of service of the notice of administration of estate on file. no confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form on file. no proposed order delivered to the court as required by local rules. deadline for curing the issues in order to avoid an automatic continuance is noon on 5/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE TRAUTMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will. No appearance required. Status hearing on close of estate will be set for 04/12/2022.
